acceptance_gates: Do not promote a source theorem unless every assumption has a Lean definition or an explicit proof-carrying field.
Do not mark bridge or certificate interfaces as the exact VdV&W theorem.
Record outer-probability, measurability, separability, envelope, tightness, and entropy-integral gaps explicitly.
Use AXLE only for auxiliary extraction/checking; local Lake remains the acceptance authority.
Keep textbook assets local-only and cite short anchors rather than long copied excerpts.
markdown_root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
notes: P11.M1 inventory: source-linked theorem cards for bracketing, VC-subgraph, and Donsker targets.  Rows are designed to prevent semantic drift before autoformalization or Lean theorem promotion.

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: measurable function class over an iid sample space
measure-backed L1(P) seminorm tying bracket width to endpoint functions
empirical measure P_n as a probability measure
outer-probability norm ||P_n - P||*_F and almost-sure GC convergence mode
next_actions: Tie the proof-carrying L1 bracketing-number witness to a measure-backed L1(P) seminorm.
Connect finite endpoint SLLN and deterministic bracket bounds into the full Theorem 2.4.1 convergence statement.
semantic_risks: Current Lean route proves the deterministic finite-bracketing handoff and sample-average endpoint assembly but still does not prove the outer almost-sure theorem.
Measurability and outer-probability bookkeeping are still abstract.
Endpoint strong-law wrappers do not yet assemble the full almost-sure uniform convergence theorem.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: A measurable function class with finite L1(P) bracketing number at every positive radius is Glivenko-Cantelli.
rows.title: Finite L1 bracketing implies Glivenko-Cantelli

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: P-measurable classes and outer expectation/probability
empirical random covering numbers over L1(P_n)
integrable envelope and truncation class F_M
outer almost-sure and outer mean convergence conclusions
next_actions: Keep as a theorem card until outer probability and random covering-number infrastructure exist.
semantic_risks: This theorem is not a bracketing theorem; mapping it into bracketing APIs would drop random entropy assumptions.
The current covering certificate only stores a future deviation proof and is not evidence for random entropy.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: A P-measurable class with integrable envelope and suitable random L1 empirical covering growth is Glivenko-Cantelli.
rows.title: Random entropy condition implies Glivenko-Cantelli

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: uniform entropy bound for all finitely discrete Q
L2(P) semimetric and F_delta measurability
empirical-process weak convergence in ell-infinity of a class
tight Brownian bridge or pre-Gaussian limit process
next_actions: Add theorem-hole cards for uniform entropy and asymptotic equicontinuity obligations before any Lean statement promotion.
semantic_risks: Donsker cannot be inferred from GC; it needs weak convergence and tightness infrastructure.
Current DonskerSpec is a stored weak-convergence statement, not a constructor from entropy.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: A measurable function class satisfying the uniform entropy bound and square-integrable envelope assumptions is P-Donsker.
rows.title: Uniform entropy condition implies P-Donsker

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: L2 and weak-L2 bracketing entropy integrals
weak second moment envelope condition
nested partitions and chaining construction
Donsker weak convergence target in function space
next_actions: Defer proof promotion until P-Donsker semantics and entropy-integral APIs exist.
semantic_risks: Bracketing GC infrastructure is insufficient for bracketing Donsker.
The current asymptotic-normality handoff consumes a Donsker proof field rather than proving it.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: Finite bracketing and covering entropy integrals plus a weak second moment envelope imply the function class is P-Donsker.
rows.title: Bracketing entropy integral implies P-Donsker

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: VC class of sets and VC index/dimension
shattering and Sauer-type growth bounds
Lr(Q) covering numbers for indicator set classes
universal-constant handling for entropy bounds
next_actions: Create theorem-hole candidates for shattering, Sauer bound, and set-class covering-number translation.
semantic_risks: Current VC route records obligations but proves no combinatorial VC theorem.
Function-class VC-subgraph results depend on this set-class entropy layer.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: VC classes of sets satisfy a universal polynomial Lr(Q) covering-number bound.
rows.title: VC set classes have polynomial covering numbers

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: subgraph set for real-valued functions
measurable envelope function
Fubini/Lebesgue-measure product argument for subgraphs
envelope-scaled covering-number statements
next_actions: Represent VC-subgraph entropy as obligations before claiming GC or Donsker consequences.
semantic_risks: Dropping envelope measurability or positivity would make the formal theorem wrong.
The existing VCDeviationCertificate is only a handoff from a supplied deviation proof.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: VC-subgraph classes with measurable envelope satisfy an envelope-scaled Lr(Q) covering bound.
rows.title: VC-subgraph function classes have envelope-scaled covering bounds

markdown_anchor.root: Math Textbook Foundation/Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process(1)/Markdown
markdown_anchor.segment: Vaart 1996 Weak Convergence and Emperical Process_101-200.md

missing_definitions: pointwise separability
P-pre-Gaussian process indexed by a class
weak second-moment envelope tail condition
Donsker constructor from VC entropy plus pre-Gaussianity
next_actions: Keep as a source-linked theorem card until the Donsker layer is formalized.
semantic_risks: This theorem has stronger assumptions than ordinary VC-subgraph GC.
Current code must not collapse VC-subgraph GC and VC-subgraph Donsker into one certificate.
rows.textbook_paraphrase: A pointwise separable, P-pre-Gaussian VC function class with a weak second-moment tail envelope is P-Donsker.
rows.title: Pointwise separable pre-Gaussian VC classes with weak envelope tail are Donsker

source: van der Vaart and Wellner, Weak Convergence and Empirical Processes
